title: Villa Barbaro at Maser plan, from I quattro libri dell'architettura
creator: Andrea Palladio (delineator); plate from I quattro libri dell'architettura
rightsholder_or_source: Metropolitan Museum of Art (Open Access); via Wikimedia Commons
research_notes: Combined sheet: plan plus elevation on one plate. High-res MET Open Access scan.
research_features: Central residential block flanked by long symmetric service wings (barchesse, agricultural arcades) ending in pavilions: a multi-building villa-farm estate on one plan. Strong axial symmetry and attached outbuildings.
feature_highlights: mirror-symmetric Palladian villa-farm parti drawn as one composition
compact central residential block of rooms around a cruciform hall
two long straight arcaded service wings (barchesse) of repeated open bays
square corner pavilions (dovecotes) terminating each wing
large semicircular garden exedra / nymphaeum behind the house
no dimension strings or scale bar; size implied by drawing and prose
supported_examples: straight orthogonal walls and junctions in the residential core
rectangular rooms around a central hall
roadmap_examples: room-purpose registry for villa and service-wing rooms

gap_features.why: one composition holds several distinct structures: the central villa plus two long service wings ending in corner pavilions/dovecotes
the barchesse are roofed but open arcaded colonnades, not fully wall-enclosed rooms
the semicircular garden exedra / nymphaeum behind the house is a true curved (apsidal) wall
the engraving carries no on-plan dimensions or labels; room identity and size come from the accompanying prose legend rather than drawn measurement
